hypothesis: If a chemically informed latent space derived from periodic table representations is used to condition a diffusion model on adsorption energy and adsorbate identity, then it will generate shell-wise surface compositions that satisfy elemental constraints and match target adsorption energy distributions more accurately than a chemically-agnostic baseline, because encoding domain-specific chemical knowledge into the autoencoder's latent space provides a structured representation that enables the diffusion model to learn meaningful composition-property relationships for inverse catalytic surface design.
